# Flow failure handling

> **Feature delivered — acceptance criteria.** Generated from the as-built BDD tests in
> [`test/stories/flows_failure_story_test.exs`](../../../echo/apps/echo_mq/test/stories/flows_failure_story_test.exs). Every scenario below is a passing
> ExUnit test driving the real `EchoMQ` surface against Valkey, so the catalogue cannot
> drift from the code.

**4 scenarios.**

## Scenario: a child that dies fails its parent by default, instead of hanging it forever

- **Given** a single-queue flow of a parent and one child, no policy declared (the default is fail-parent)
- **When** the child exhausts its retries and lands in the morgue
- **Then** the parent is failed too — moved to dead, the dead child recorded in its :failed

## Scenario: a child marked ignore-on-failure lets the parent proceed once its siblings finish

- **Given** a flow of a parent and two children, one marked ignore_dependency_on_failure
- **When** the ignored child dies and the other child completes
- **Then** the parent is released (not failed), and reads the ignored failure and the completed result apart

## Scenario: a parent with no ignored failures reads an empty map

- **Then** ignored_failures returns an empty map for a parent that never ignored a failure

## Scenario: many flows are submitted in one call, fail-closed per flow

- **Given** two well-formed flows
- **When** they are added in bulk
- **Then** both parents land, each held awaiting its child
